# Feature: Interactive crawl escalate

<!-- auto-generated by /implement on 2026-07-17 — do not edit manually unless correcting intent -->

## Intent
Upgrade AutoGuide’s own Playwright crawl beyond single `goto`: safe interactions (links/buttons) produce ordered multi-step flow traces. Respect `safeMode` / `isSafeAction`. Crawl is the autonomy fallback when host Playwright JSON is absent.

Roadmap: Autonomy batch B — depends on #144 sufficiency gate.

## Preconditions
- Playwright chromium available for integration tests
- Fixture HTML or local HTTP server for crawl

## Happy Path
- [ ] Crawl visits route then clicks safe interactive elements
- [ ] Traces/flows have ≥2 steps when page has safe clickables
- [ ] Destructive labels still skipped via `isSafeAction`
- [ ] Unit/integration tests with fixture HTML
- [ ] Docs note crawl as autonomy fallback (not host-test requirement)

## Edge Cases
- [ ] Pages with only destructive controls → goto-only trace
- [ ] Playwright unavailable → empty result (existing behavior)
- [ ] safeMode=false allows destructive labels

## Regression
- [ ] Existing `isSafeAction` tests still pass
- [ ] Scan `--crawl` still merges crawl traces into flows

## Assumptions
- Cap safe clicks per route (YAGNI constant) to keep crawl bounded
- Issue C wires sufficiency→auto escalate; this slice upgrades crawl itself

| Step | Filename |
|------|----------|
| n/a | CLI/Playwright only |

## Implementation Notes
- `packages/playwright/src/crawl.ts` — interactive safe clicks after goto; `filterSafeClickCandidates`
- Cap: `CRAWL_MAX_SAFE_CLICKS = 5`; destructive labels still via `isSafeAction`
- Tests: `crawl.interactive.test.ts` with fixture HTML server
- Docs: `docs/api/cli.md` — crawl is autonomy fallback, not host-test requirement
